markers.source_table: literature (Dobrican et al. 2022; Siddhuraj et al. 2021)

markers.evidence_type: chymase, connective tissue mast cell marker
markers.source_table: literature (Dobrican et al. 2022; Harvima et al. 1997)

markers.evidence_type: carboxypeptidase A3, mast cell marker, high in skin MCTC
CD117, stem cell factor receptor, mast cell surface marker
high-affinity IgE receptor, mast cell marker

other_findings.finding: Skin mast cells are predominantly MCTC (connective tissue type) expressing tryptase, chymase and CPA3
other_findings.source_table: Dobrican et al. 2022; Siddhuraj et al. 2021

other_findings.finding: Mast cells are located in superficial and deep dermis, preferentially perivascular and periaxonal
Skin mast cells have very high CPA3 staining intensity compared to lung and gut
MC_TC cells (tryptase+chymase+) are the majority of mast cells in skin, with MCT (tryptase only) being a minority

evidence_quotes.quote: The majority of tryptase-rich mast cells are predominantly located in mucous membranes (T-mast cells), while those containing tryptase, carboxypeptidase and chymase predominate in the connective tissue of the skin (TC-mast cells)
Mast cells in patients with urticaria, similar to those in healthy individuals, are located in the superficial and deep dermis, preferentially in the perivascular and periaxonal spaces
